steps.title: Simplify the given equation to standard form
steps.content:
The given equation is $3x = 45y - 27$.
Dividing both sides by 3:
$$x = 15y - 9$$

steps.title: Identify what form the second equation must have
steps.content: A system has no solution when the equations are parallel (same slope, different y-intercept). Since we have $x = 15y - 9$, the second equation must be of the form $x = 15y + k$ where $k \neq -9$.

steps.title: Test the choices to find the parallel form
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($\frac{1}{3}x = 5y$):
Multiply both sides by 3:
$$x = 15y$$
This is the form $x = 15y + k$ with $k = 0 \neq -9$, so these lines are parallel with no solution. That matches choice B — B is correct.
